name: claude-hooks
description: Claude Code hooks configuration specialist. Use when creating hooks for
qualityReason: Slight overlap with other hook skills
capabilities: Create PreToolUse validation hooks to validate tool inputs before execution
Set up PostToolUse logging hooks to record tool outputs and file changes
Configure notification hooks for custom alerts and integrations
Implement Stop hooks for cleanup tasks and final reports
Document hook patterns and matcher configurations
Provide environment variable references for hook scripts
author: Joseph OBrien
securitySummary: This skill contains only markdown documentation files with hook configuration templates. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or file system access. Static findings are false positives caused by the analyzer detecting example shell command syntax in documentation, not actual executing code.
valueStatement: Claude Code hooks enable automated validation, logging, and notifications during AI tool execution. This skill provides templates and best practices for configuring PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Notification, and Stop hooks.
userTitle: Configure Claude Code hooks for automation
limitations: This skill provides reference documentation only, not executable hook code
Users must manually configure hooks in ~/.claude/settings.json
Hook commands must be written and tested by the user

useCases.title: Audit tool execution
useCases.description: Log all Claude Code tool calls for compliance auditing and security review

useCases.title: Enforce coding standards
useCases.description: Validate code changes before execution to enforce team conventions

useCases.title: Custom notifications
useCases.description: Send alerts to external systems when specific tools complete or fail

promptTemplates.title: Create validation hook
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a PreToolUse hook that validates Bash commands before they run

promptTemplates.title: Set up logging
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a PostToolUse hook that logs all file write operations to a file

promptTemplates.title: Notification integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure a Notification hook that sends alerts to Slack when Claude completes tasks

promptTemplates.title: Matcher patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain the different matcher patterns I can use to target specific tools like Bash(git:*) or MCP tools

bestPractices: Keep hooks fast (under 1 second) to avoid blocking Claude Code execution
Use specific matcher patterns instead of wildcards to target only needed tools
Handle errors gracefully with proper shell error handling to prevent cascade failures
antiPatterns: Using verbose logging in hooks that adds excessive output to the conversation context
Creating hooks that block indefinitely without timeout handling
Configuring hooks for all tools (*) when only specific tools need automation

faq.question: Which Claude versions support hooks?
faq.answer: Hooks require Claude Code version 0.9.0 or later. Check your installation with 'claude --version'.

faq.question: What is the maximum hook execution time?
faq.answer: Hooks should complete in under 1 second. Long-running hooks will block Claude Code tool execution.

faq.question: How do hooks integrate with MCP tools?
faq.answer: Use matcher pattern 'mcp__*' to target all MCP tools, or specific MCP tool names for individual tools.

faq.question: Are hooks secure?
faq.answer: Hooks run as your user with your permissions. Only install hooks from trusted sources and review commands before use.

faq.question: Why is my hook not triggering?
faq.answer: Check that your settings.json has correct JSON syntax, the event name matches (PreToolUse, PostToolUse), and matcher patterns are valid.

faq.question: How do hooks differ from custom instructions?
faq.answer: Hooks execute shell commands automatically on events, while custom instructions affect Claude's behavior through prompt context.
